title: CHAPTER 3 —
MARKS AND CHANNELS
segments.body_html:

Chapter 3 — Marks and Channels — This grammar was first named systematically by Jacques Bertin in Sémiologie Graphique in 1967. Bertin was a French carto

beats.text: Every chart you have ever seen is built from the same small set of pieces. There are only a handful of geometric primitives , the marks , and only a handful of visual properties through which those marks can vary , the channels. That is the whole vocabulary. Every scatterplot, bar chart, choropleth, and bubble chart is a specific combination of marks assigned specific channels, each channel carrying a specific attribute from the data. This grammar was first named systematically by Jacques Bertin in Sémiologie Graphique in 1967.

segments.label: 01 / 13 · HOOK

title: MARKS:
THE GEOMETRIC PRIMITIVES
segments.body_html: mark→Point marks→Line marks→Area marks→Glyph marks

beats.text: A mark is a geometric element used to represent data. There are four types: Point marks , single symbols, dots, circles. Each point typically represents one observation. Scatterplots are built from point marks.

segments.label: 02 / 13 · MARKS: THE GEOMETRIC PRIMITIVE

title: CHANNELS:
THE VISUAL VARIABLES
segments.body_html:
Positionwhere on the x-axis, where on the y-axis
Lengthhow long, how tall
Areahow large the enclosing region is
Color huewhat color (red vs. blue vs. green)
Color luminancehow light or dark

beats.text: A channel is a property of a mark that can vary to encode data. The major channels are: Position , where on the x-axis, where on the y-axis Length , how long, how tall Area , how large the enclosing region is Color hue , what color (red vs. blue vs. green) Color luminance , how light or dark Shape , circle vs. square vs. triangle Orientation , the angle of a line or symbol

segments.label: 03 / 13 · CHANNELS: THE VISUAL VARIABLES

title: WHY AREA GETS UNDERESTIMATED:
STEVENS' POWER LAW
segments.body_html:
1.7 times
the original — not 2 times
→
1.5 times
the original

beats.text: The mechanism behind the ranking is a psychophysical finding from Stanley Smith Stevens in 1957. Stevens showed that human perception of physical magnitudes follows a power function: where Ψ is perceived intensity, I is actual physical intensity, k is a scaling constant, and a is an exponent that varies by the type of stimulus.

segments.label: 04 / 13 · WHY AREA GETS UNDERESTIMATED:

title: MAGNITUDE CHANNELS AND
IDENTITY CHANNELS
segments.body_html: Magnitude channels→Identity channels→expressiveness principle→effectiveness principle

beats.text: Munzner's framework adds one more cut that I find clarifying. The channels divide into two families: Magnitude channels are suited for quantitative data , data that has a meaningful order and size. Position, length, area, luminance. These channels convey "more" and "less" in a way the reader can decode.

segments.label: 05 / 13 · MAGNITUDE CHANNELS AND IDENTIT

title: THREE WORKED EXAMPLES FROM BEFORE THE
FRAMEWORK HAD A NAME.
segments.body_html: John Snow's 1854 cholera map of London.→Charles Joseph Minard's 1869 Napoleon campaign.→Florence Nightingale's 1858 polar area chart.

beats.text: The framework is abstract. Three charts make it concrete. All three were made before Bertin named the grammar they were using. John Snow's 1854 cholera map of London. Snow's question was: where in the Soho neighborhood are the cholera deaths concentrated? His most important attribute was location , two-dimensional, geographic, continuous. He used position-on-map to encode it. Each death is a point mark; its (x, y) coordinates are the address. Nothing else needed. The cluster around the Broad Street pump is visible immediately.

segments.label: 06 / 13 · THREE WORKED EXAMPLES FROM BEF

segments.section: HOW THIS CHANGES THE PROMPT YO
title: HOW THIS CHANGES THE
PROMPT YOU WRITE.
segments.body_html:
The marks-and-channels framework is not only a lens for reading charts. It is a specification language for building them

How This Changes the Prompt You Write — When you ask Claude Code to "make a box plot of these AI capability scores," Claude Code will produce something that loo

beats.text: The marks-and-channels framework is not only a lens for reading charts. It is a specification language for building them. When you ask Claude Code to "make a box plot of these AI capability scores," Claude Code will produce something that looks like a box plot. It may show the full range instead of the interquartile range. It may size the outliers as ticks instead of points. It may scale the y-axis to the data range rather than the meaningful range.

segments.label: 07 / 13 · HOW THIS CHANGES THE PROMPT YO

title: THE FEYNMAN
TEST.
segments.body_html:
·What are the marks?
·What channels are those marks using to vary?
·What data attribute does each channel encode?
·For each quantitative attribute, is the channel a magnitude channel?
·For each categorical attribute, is the channel an identity channel?

beats.text: Feynman had a test for understanding: if you can teach something to a freshman, you understand it. The lecture is over when the student can work the problem, not just recognize the answer. The test for this chapter is simpler. Pick a chart you saw today , a newspaper, a dashboard, a research paper, anything. Give yourself ninety seconds to answer these questions:

segments.label: 08 / 13 · THE FEYNMAN TEST

title: EXERCISES
segments.body_html:
·A bubble chart where x-position is GDP per capita, y-position is life expectancy, bubble area is population, and bubble
·A choropleth map where polygon fill luminance encodes unemployment rate.
·A scatterplot where point shape (circle, square, triangle) encodes year (2020, 2021, 2022).

beats.text: Exercise 3.1 , Channel identification. For each of the following charts, identify (a) the marks, (b) the channels in use, (c) the data attribute each channel encodes, and (d) whether each channel-attribute pairing satisfies the expressiveness principle. A bubble chart where x-position is GDP per capita, y-position is life expectancy, bubble area is population, and bubble hue is continent. A choropleth map where polygon fill luminance encodes unemployment rate. A scatterplot where point shape (circle, square, triangle) encodes year (2020, 2021, 2022).

segments.label: 09 / 13 · EXERCISES

title: KEY TERMS
segments.body_html: Mark.→Channel.→Magnitude channel.→Identity channel.→Expressiveness principle (Munzner).

beats.text: Mark. A geometric primitive (point, line, area, glyph) used to represent data. Channel. A visual variable (position, length, area, hue, luminance, shape, orientation) that encodes a data attribute by varying.

They define the stack, naming conventions, color system, and typography the figures use.
